text:
Tom had always wanted a giant owl. Somehow, he convinced his parents to buy him one. It wasn't just any owl—it was an enormous Great Eagle owl.

Every night, Tom would sneak into the attic and talk to his owl. The owl would swoop down like a hawk, catching insects. Tom taught it tricks like catching fish out of a lake. They had a great time.

One day, Tom noticed his owl acting peculiar. It wouldn't perch. It wouldn't perch anything. Tom worried.

After some observation, he realized his owl had fallen sick. Tom was devastated. His owl was his best friend.

He spent months looking after it. Eventually, it recovered. Tom's parents thought it was just a funny story.

Years later, Tom climbed into the attic again. He spotted his old buddy. But this time, the owl wasn't just sleeping—it was curled up dead.

Tom sat there feeling cold all over.

Then he remembered that bird flu had swept through the neighborhood
